description.p: A set of 12 standard metal rounds for use with the Cannon Menmonita. These are basic ammunition with no special properties beyond their ability to be fired from the cannon.

When this yellow-tinged cannon round hits a target, it detonates in a brilliant display of lightning. Each creature in a 15-foot radius of the impact must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 6d6 lightning damage, or half as much on a successful one.

This golden round marks a target and calls down a lightning strike. The creature hit by this ammunition is marked, and a massive lightning bolt strikes it on your next turn (no action required). The marked creature takes 8d6 lightning damage, and cannot be marked again until you finish a short rest.

This black round creates a massive explosion on impact. All creatures in a 15-foot radius must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 bludgeoning damage on a failed save (half on success). Those that fail are also knocked back 15 feet and knocked prone.

When fired, this green round detonates in a burst of dark magic. A bone golem is summoned in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of the impact. The golem acts on your turn and obeys your commands. It remains for 1 hour or until reduced to 0 hit points, after which it crumbles to dust. Once this ammunition is used, you cannot use this effect again until you finish a long rest.

This crimson round ignites into a devastating inferno on impact. Each creature in a 15-foot radius must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 fire damage on a failed save (half on success). The affected area becomes difficult terrain covered in flames for 1 minute.

This blue-hued round's properties remain mysterious. When fired, the wielder should consult with the Dungeon Master regarding its effects, as its true power has yet to be fully discovered or documented.
